column28: Verified customer concern, vehicle experiences a no-crank condition when hot. I found a large voltage drop at the ground strap when testing. Recommend replacing damaged ground strap first in order to continue this diagnosis, this may fix the issue but in order to move forward this must be addressed first.
column29: Alright for a lean code: first I would start with any vacuum leaks, visual inspections, spraying carb cleaner near suspected leak points to isolate any changes and engine RPM may indicate a leak, second I would check vaccume solenoids for actuation electrically as well as mechanicaly able to hold vacuum, third I would check MAF sensors by simply allowing the vehicle to idle and disconnecting the sensor, if no change? That’s the issue, if change in RMp, the sensor was good. Finally if all those other issues are not present I would check fuel trims compared to the factory spec of what they’re supposed to be. With these tests I’m positive I can isolate what’s going on

column28: Verified Concern: Vehicle has an intermittent no-crank condition when hot.Findings: Performed voltage drop testing and found excessive voltage drop at the engine ground strap under load.Recommendation: Recommend repairing/replacing the affected ground connection and retesting starting system operation.
column29: 1. Perform smoke/boost leak test — good: system holds pressure; bad: leaks at hoses, intercooler, PCV, or intake connections. 2. Check low side fuel supply volume/pressure under load — good: pressure remains stable; bad: pressure drops under acceleration/load. 3. Verify MAF readings and compare to calculated load — good: readings match expected airflow; bad: skewed or contaminated readings. 4. Review fuel trims at idle and under load — good: trims stay within normal range; bad: high positive trims indicating unmetered air or fuel delivery issue.
column30: Verify LIN idle voltage is near battery voltage and check for proper dominant/recessive switching with a scope during mirror commands. Confirm LIN frames are present and compare signal activity to SI specifications. Isolate the circuit at splice points or disconnect nodes one at a time to determine if the mirror node, wiring, or master module is pulling the bus down or not communicating.
